---
title: "Per-Component Parameter Schemas"
---

## Overview

Phase 0.0 of the DeepLearning_Widget refactoring replaced the monolithic `DecoderParams`, `EncoderParams`, and `PostEncoderModuleParams` structs with per-component parameter structs. Each struct is a plain reflect-cpp struct containing only the user-configurable fields for its specific component.

These structs are complemented by companion `*ParamSchemas.hpp` headers that provide `ParameterUIHints` specializations for richer auto-generated UI forms.

## Architecture

```
DeepLearning library (src/DeepLearning/)
├── channel_decoding/
│   ├── ChannelDecoder.hpp         ← DecoderContext + per-decoder param structs
│   └── DecoderParamSchemas.hpp    ← ParameterUIHints for decoder params
├── channel_encoding/
│   ├── ChannelEncoder.hpp         ← EncoderContext + per-encoder param structs
│   └── EncoderParamSchemas.hpp    ← ParameterUIHints for encoder params
└── post_encoder/
    ├── PostEncoderModuleFactory.hpp  ← GlobalAvgPoolModuleParams + SpatialPointModuleParams
    └── PostEncoderParamSchemas.hpp   ← ParameterUIHints for post-encoder params
```

## Context vs. Parameter Structs

Each component separates **runtime context** (set by SlotAssembler, not user-configurable) from **user parameters** (exposed via ParameterSchema for auto-generated UI forms):

| Component | Context Struct | User Param Struct(s) |
|---|---|---|
| Decoders | `DecoderContext` | `MaskDecoderParams`, `PointDecoderParams`, `LineDecoderParams`, `FeatureVectorDecoderParams` |
| Encoders | `EncoderContext` | `ImageEncoderParams`, `Point2DEncoderParams`, `Mask2DEncoderParams`, `Line2DEncoderParams` |
| Post-Encoder | `ImageSize` (passed separately) | `GlobalAvgPoolModuleParams`, `SpatialPointModuleParams` |

## Using extractParameterSchema

The `ParameterSchema` dependency enables `extractParameterSchema<T>()` on all per-component param structs:

```cpp
#include "channel_decoding/DecoderParamSchemas.hpp"

auto schema = WhiskerToolbox::Transforms::V2::extractParameterSchema<dl::MaskDecoderParams>();
// schema.fields[0].name == "threshold"
// schema.fields[0].type_name == "float"
// schema.fields[0].tooltip == "Binary threshold for mask generation"
```

For encoder params, `RasterMode` is automatically detected as an `enum` type:

```cpp
#include "channel_encoding/EncoderParamSchemas.hpp"

auto schema = WhiskerToolbox::Transforms::V2::extractParameterSchema<dl::Point2DEncoderParams>();
// schema.fields[0].name == "mode"
// schema.fields[0].type_name == "enum"
// schema.fields[0].allowed_values == {"Binary", "Heatmap"}  (restricted by ParameterUIHints)
```

## ParameterUIHints Pattern

Following the `ProcessingOptionsSchema.hpp` pattern used by `MediaProcessingPipeline`, UI annotations are kept in companion headers:

```cpp
// channel_decoding/DecoderParamSchemas.hpp
template<>
struct ParameterUIHints<dl::MaskDecoderParams> {
    static void annotate(ParameterSchema & schema) {
        if (auto * f = schema.field("threshold")) {
            f->tooltip = "Binary threshold for mask generation";
            f->min_value = 0.01;
            f->max_value = 1.0;
            f->is_exclusive_min = true;
        }
    }
};
```

These hints are automatically applied when `extractParameterSchema<T>()` is called,
since the template function invokes `ParameterUIHints<T>::annotate(schema)` as its
final step. Include the appropriate `*ParamSchemas.hpp` header to ensure the
specialization is visible at the point of schema extraction.

## SlotAssembler Integration

`SlotAssembler` bridges the widget-level flat binding data (`DeepLearningBindingData.hpp`) with the per-component param structs:

- `encodeDynamicSlot()` constructs `EncoderContext` + the appropriate per-encoder params from `SlotBindingData` flat fields.
- `decodeOutputs()` / `decodeOutputsToBuffer()` construct `DecoderContext` + per-decoder params from `OutputBindingData` flat fields.
- `configurePostEncoderModule()` converts the string-based `interpolation` parameter to `InterpolationMode` and constructs `SpatialPointModuleParams` internally.

The PIMPL firewall is preserved — `SlotAssembler.hpp` does not expose any torch-adjacent types.
